name: 1Password
price_basis: advertised Individual plan, checked 2026-07 after the March 2026 price change
verdict_reason: KEEP: a rebuild bug here does not cost you a file, it costs you every account you own.
what_zo_builds: Nothing that touches the vault. Rolling your own secret store is how people end up with plaintext passwords in a markdown file. Around it, Zo builds an automation that cross-checks your subscription ledger for accounts worth closing, a quarterly reminder to run Watchtower and refresh your emergency kit, and a standing rule that credentials never land in files or logs. The cryptography stays with people who do cryptography.
dont_kill_if: The only honest exit is sideways, to another audited manager or a free built-in like Apple Passwords if you live on one platform. Rolling your own is not an exit, it is an incident.
build_brief: Goal: keep 1Password and audit around it. Do not ask me to export my vault into plain files. Build /security/audit.md: read my subscription ledger and list every service with a login I no longer pay for, so I can close those accounts. Add a quarterly automation that reminds me to check my 1Password emergency kit and run Watchtower. Add a permanent rule that you never store passwords in files, logs, or notes. Done when the first audit lists my dead accounts and the reminder fires on schedule.
